pages.text:
Reasoning
Q1
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Six people(P, Q, R, S, T and U) are going to the
market one after another,but not in the same
order. Two people go to the market between S
and P. P goes before Q. Two people go between
R and U. U goes after P. R goes immediately
before T. S goes before P.
 
How many people are going to the market
between R and Q?
(A) 5
(B) 4
(C) 3
(D) 2
(E) None of these
Q2
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Six people(P, Q, R, S, T and U) are going to the
market one after another,but not in the same
order. Two people go to the market between S
and P. P goes before Q. Two people go between
R and U. U goes after P. R goes immediately
before T. S goes before P.
 
Who goes immediately before U?
(A) T
(B) R
(C) S
(D) P
(E) None of these
Q3
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
 
Six people(P, Q, R, S, T and U) are going to the
market one after another,but not in the same
order. Two people go to the market between S
and P. P goes before Q. Two people go between
R and U. U goes after P. R goes immediately
before T. S goes before P.
 
Who is going immediately after S?
(A) T
(B) R
(C) U
(D) Q
(E) None of these
Q4
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below.
 
Six flowers R, B, M, X, K and L are planted in a
row of a garden according to their height in
descending order from left to right. Only two
flowers are shorter than flower X. There are two
flowers planted between flower M and flower X.
Flower R is shorter than flower K but taller than
flower B which is not the shortest.
 
Which among the following is the 2nd tallest
flower?
(A) Flower M
(B) Flower K
(C) Flower L
(D) Flower B
(E) None of these
Q5
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below.
 
Six flowers R, B, M, X, K and L are planted in a
row of a garden according to their height in
descending order from left to right. Only two
flowers are shorter than flower X. There are two
flowers planted between flower M and flower X.
Flower R is shorter than flower K but taller than
flower B which is not the shortest.
 
Which among the following is the tallest?
(A) Flower M
(B) Flower K
 
Order Ranking

(C) Flower L
(D) Flower B
(E) None of these
Q6
Directions:Consider the following information
and answer the questions accordingly.
 
8 persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H have different
heights. A is taller than only 2 other persons and
one of them is C. Only 3 persons are taller than C
but shorter than F. Only 1 person has height
between F and E. B is taller than D who is taller
than G. G is taller than H. H is not the shortest.
 
Who among the following is shorter than D but
taller than G?
(A) G
(B) E
(C) F
(D) Both F and E
(E) Both E and H
Q7
Directions:Consider the following information
and answer the questions accordingly.
 
8 persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H have different
heights. A is taller than only 2 other persons and
one of them is C. Only 3 persons are taller than C
but shorter than F. Only 1 person has height
between F and E. B is taller than D who is taller
than G. G is taller than H. H is not the shortest.
 
Who is the 4 tallest?
(A) F
(B) D
(C) G
(D) E
(E) C
Q8
Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Seven friends have different height. Raju is taller
than atleast four people. Manu is taller than Raju
but shorter than Kamal. Tanuj is 170cm tall.
Dinesh is taller than gagan but shorter than
tanuj. Gagan is 165cm tall and is taller than
Mohan.
 
What could be the possible sum of heights of
Tanuj and Kamal?
(A) 270cm
(B) 360cm
(C) 210cm
(D) 320cm
(E) 325 cm
Q9
Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Seven friends have different height. Raju is taller
than atleast four people. Manu is taller than Raju
but shorter than Kamal. Tanuj is 170cm tall.
Dinesh is taller than gagan but shorter than
tanuj. Gagan is 165cm tall and is taller than
Mohan.
 
Who among the following is/are taller than
Gagan?
I. Raju
II. Dinesh
III. Mohan
(A) I and II
(B) II and III
(C) All I,II and III
(D) I and III
(E) Only III
Q10
Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Seven people - L, M, R, T, V, W and Y have
different number of coins with them. The number
of people having more coins than M is same as
the number of people having less coins than him.
R has more coins than L but less than Y. T has
more coins than V but not the highest. Y has less
coins than V.
 
Who among the following has the highest
number of coins?
(A) W
(B) V
(C) Y
(D) M
(E) None of these
Q11
 
th

Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Seven people - L, M, R, T, V, W and Y have
different number of coins with them. The number
of people having more coins than M is same as
the number of people having less coins than him.
R has more coins than L but less than Y. T has
more coins than V but not the highest. Y has less
coins than V.
 
If the number of coins with people decreases in
alphabetical order, then the order for how many
people remains the same?
(A) 2
(B) 3
(C) 1
(D) 4
(E) None
Q12
Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Six people have different ages. Aman is elder to
Esha but younger to Harish. At least two people
are elder to Harish. Uday is elder to Priya. Jatin is
younger to Aman but elder to at least one
person.
 
As many people are elder to Jatin as are younger
to _____
(A) Uday
(B) Priya
(C) Aman
(D) Harish
(E) None of these
Q13
Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Six people have different ages. Aman is elder to
Esha but younger to Harish. At least two people
are elder to Harish. Uday is elder to Priya. Jatin is
younger to Aman but elder to at least one
person.
 
How many people are younger to Priya?
(A) 4
(B) 5
(C) 3
(D) 2
(E) 1
Q14
Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Six people in a class scored different marks.
Manoj scored more marks than Rahul. Anjali
scored 25 marks which is the third lowest marks.
Kartik scored 57 marks which is more than the
marks scored by Manoj. Pinku scored less than
Rahul but more than Neetu.
 
How many people scored less than Manoj?
(A) 3
(B) 4
(C) 5
(D) 2
(E) 1
Q15
Consider the following information and answer
the questions accordingly.
 
Six people in a class scored different marks.
Manoj scored more marks than Rahul. Anjali
scored 25 marks which is the third lowest marks.
Kartik scored 57 marks which is more than the
marks scored by Manoj. Pinku scored less than
Rahul but more than Neetu.
 
Which of the following could be the possible
score of Rahul?
(A) 20
(B) 18
(C) 60
(D) 55
(E) 15
Q16
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Six persons- P, Q, R, S, T and V are having
different Electricity Bills and are arranged in
descending order. Q’s electricity bill is only more
than T. Third highest bill is 3500. R’s bill is more
than V who has more bills than S. Only two
persons are having bills between R and P. V’s
Electricity bill has more than P's.
If the sum of the Bills of S and V is 6500 and the
sum of the bill of S and R is 9000 then what is

the bill of R?
(A) 3500
(B) 6500
(C) 6000
(D) 4500
(E) 2000
Q17
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Six persons- P, Q, R, S, T and V are having
different Electricity Bills and are arranged in
descending order. Q’s electricity bill is only more
than T. Third highest bill is 3500. R’s bill is more
than V who has more bills than S. Only two
persons are having bills between R and P. V’s
Electricity bill has more than P's.
Who among the following has an Electricity bill
less than S?
(A) Q
(B) V
(C) R
(D) T
(E) Both Q & T
Q18
Directions:Consider the following information
and answer the questions accordingly.
 
Seven students M, N, O, P, Q, R and S are
arranged in descending order of their weights for
the medical examination. Only two students are
lighter than N. O and Q are heavier than N but
lighter than M. R is lighter than S, but heavier
than P. Three students are heavier than R, but
lighter than O. S, whose weight is 80kg is lighter
than Q. The weight of second lightest person is
55kg.
 
What is the possible weight of N?
(A) 50kg
(B) 85kg
(C) 90kg
(D) 65kg
(E) 82kg
Q19
Directions:Consider the following information
and answer the questions accordingly.
 
Seven students M, N, O, P, Q, R and S are
arranged in descending order of their weights for
the medical examination. Only two students are
lighter than N. O and Q are heavier than N but
lighter than M. R is lighter than S, but heavier
than P. Three students are heavier than R, but
lighter than O. S, whose weight is 80kg is lighter
than Q. The weight of second lightest person is
55kg.
 
Who among the following is the heaviest person
among the students?
(A) S
(B) P
(C) R
(D) N
(E) M
Q20
Directions:Consider the following information
and answer the questions accordingly.
 
Seven students M, N, O, P, Q, R and S are
arranged in descending order of their weights for
the medical examination. Only two students are
lighter than N. O and Q are heavier than N but
lighter than M. R is lighter than S, but heavier
than P. Three students are heavier than R, but
lighter than O. S, whose weight is 80kg is lighter
than Q. The weight of second lightest person is
55kg.
 
If all the students are arranged according to the
alphabetical order from top to bottom, then who
among the following weighs 55kg?
(A) O
(B) P
(C) Q
(D) R
(E) S

Q1

CASE 2

is no longer valid.
CASE 1

Q4
Text Solution:
Only two flowers are shorter than flower X.
There are two flowers planted between flower M
and flower X.
M > __ > __ > X > __ > __
Flower R is shorter than flower K but taller than
flower B which is not the shortest.
M > K > R > X > B > __
We know, L is one of the flowers so the final
arrangement is-
M > K > R > X > B > L
Flower K is the 2nd tallest flower.
Q5
Text Solution:
Only two flowers are shorter than flower X.
There are two flowers planted between flower M
and flower X.

M > __ > __ > X > __ > __
Flower R is shorter than flower K but taller than
flower B which is not the shortest.
M > K > R > X > B > __
We know, L is one of the flowers so the final
arrangement is-
M > K > R > X > B > L
Flower M is the tallest flower.

Q10
Text Solution:
The number of people having more coins than M
is same as the number of people having less
coins than him.
_____ > _____ > _____ > M > _____ > _____ >
______
R has more coins than L but less than Y.
Y > R > L
T has more coins than V but not the highest. Y
has less coins than V.
W > T > V > M > Y > R > L
W has the highest number of coins.
Q11
Text Solution:
The number of people having more coins than M
is same as the number of people having less
coins than him.
_____ > _____ > _____ > M > _____ > _____ >
______
R has more coins than L but less than Y.
Y > R > L
T has more coins than V but not the highest. Y
has less coins than V.
W > T > V > M > Y > R > L
After arranging in alphabetical order, we get:
L > M > R > T > V > W > Y
So, none of the persons has the same order.

Q16
Text Solution:
S + V= 6500
S= 6500 - 3500
= 3000
S + R= 9000
R= 9000 - 3000

= 6000
Q’s electricity bill is only more than T.
R’s bill is more than V who has more than S. Only
two persons are having bills in between R and P.
V’s Electricity bill has more than P.
Third highest bill is 3500.

Q18
Text Solution:
Only two students are lighter than N. Here 7 for
heaviest and 1 for lightest person.
O and Q are heavier than N but lighter than M.
Three students are heavier than R, but lighter
than O.
Thus, case 1 cancelled.
R is lighter than S, but heavier than P.
Thus, case 2 cancelled.
O and Q are heavier than N but lighter than M.

S, whose weight is 80kg is lighter than Q.
The weight of second lightest person is 55kg.
The weight of N must lie between 55kg and
80kg, so the possible weight of N is 65kg.
Q19
Text Solution:
Only two students are lighter than N. Here 7 for
heaviest and 1 for lightest person.
O and Q are heavier than N but lighter than M.
Three students are heavier than R, but lighter
than O.
Thus, case 1 cancelled.
R is lighter than S, but heavier than P.
Thus, case 2 cancelled.

O and Q are heavier than N but lighter than M.
S, whose weight is 80kg is lighter than Q.
The weight of second lightest person is 55kg.
M is the heaviest person among the students.
